mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, which increases present values and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day S&P 500 returns following three-month FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over two consecutive months signals accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate and a rise in the consumer price index produce a restrictive real monetary environment that compresses equity valuation multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not produce a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is insignificant in a regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand spillovers that reduce corporate earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle months, declines in HOUST are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or the t-statistic of the return difference between HOUST-decline and HOUST-rise periods is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production directly predicts positive near-term S&P 500 returns because higher factory output signals stronger corporate revenue growth and operating leverage over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO fail to generate positive mean 5-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in retail sales exceeding two percent indicates a consumer demand contraction that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings estimates are cut.
failure_condition: Triggered instances do not produce negative mean 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling nonfarm payrolls and falling industrial production jointly signals a broad economic contraction that produces strongly negative S&P 500 returns beyond what either variable predicts independently over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and INDPRO changes does not significantly predict 20-day returns (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression, or the combined signal produces positive returns in more than 45% of joint-down months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above five percent restricts credit availability and raises corporate borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days as leveraged firms face margin pressure.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly negative compared to periods below 5.0% (t-test p > 0.05).
